The separate Wikimedia Endowment reported net assets of 169.4 million dollars, up roughly 25 million year over year.

A June 2025 trial of "Simple Article Summaries" was paused after one day following editor backlash, which deepened distrust about AI priorities."},{"url":"https://medium.com/regarding-wikipedia/wikipedia-is-doing-the-capitalist-thing-56a393232943","title":"Big Tech’s Anti-Labor Playbook Has Come for Wikipedia | by Jake Orlowitz | Regarding Wikipedia | May, 2026 | Medium","content":"Wikipedia’s editors are now threatening to strike in solidarity.
Wikimedia Enterprise, the team that provides high speed, high volume API access to AI labs, just turned profitable on $8.3 million in revenue, a 148% jump from the prior year.
Bernadette Meehan became CEO on January 20, 2026, recruited from a career that included Wall Street stints at J.P.
Four months in, the longtime lead developer of MediaWiki is fired, the team that personifies community service is dissolved, and the union is in open confrontation.
This is the standard tech playbook."},{"url":"https://www.linkedin.com/posts/mike-schoonover_hundreds-of-prolific-wikipedia-editors-are-activity-7470363148978393088-yqxt","title":"Wikimedia Foundation Fires Tech Team Amid Union Tensions | Mike Schoonover posted on the topic | LinkedIn","content":"Six people: five engineers and one manager.

The Foundation's response: the layoffs are unrelated to the union.
One of the biggest misconceptions in web development today is that you need Laravel, React, Node.js, or an entirely new tech stack to integrate AI into your application."},{"url":"https://www.theregister.com/software/2026/05/30/wikipedia-editors-plot-strike-and-banner-sabotage-after-wikimedia-layoffs/5248491","title":"Wikipedia editors plot strike and banner sabotage after Wikimedia layoffs","content":"The Wikimedia Foundation (WMF) has sparked a revolt among Wikipedia editors after disbanding the engineering team responsible for many community-requested fixes and moderation tools.
It said the decision came after months of internal reviews that started last year.
One of the laid-off engineers created the union page on Wikimedia Meta earlier this month, fueling accusations from some editors that the restructuring amounted to union busting."},{"url":"https://timesofindia.indiatimes.com/technology/tech-news/hundreds-of-wikipedia-editors-are-threatening-to-go-on-strike-and-the-reason-is-these-engineers/articleshow/131394771.cms","title":"Hundreds of Wikipedia editors are threatening to go on strike and the reason is these engineers - The Times of India","content":"Hundreds of Wikipedia editors are threatening to take major action against the company.
The team was responsible for developing and maintaining tools used by contributors, including plagiarism detection systems, dark mode features, and charting tools.The decision has reportedly triggered an immediate backlash from volunteer editors, some of whom also raised concerns about whether the move was linked to ongoing unionisation efforts among Wikimedia staff.
More than 700 editors have since signed a petition expressing support for collective action if requested by the union, Wiki Workers United (WWU), the report adds."}]}

tavily.raw: {"query":"Wikimedia Foundation Brooke Vibber dismissal Community Tech team layoffs editor strike 2026","follow_up_questions":null,"answer":null,"images":[],"results":[{"url":"https://www.metaintro.com/blog/wikipedia-editors-threaten-strike-wikimedia-layoffs-2026","title":"Why Wikipedia Editors Are Threatening to... | Metaintro","content":"# Why Wikipedia Editors Are Threatening to Strike Over Layoffs in 2026. Roughly 600 Wikipedia editors signed a solidarity petition after Wikimedia cut 6 staff and fired MediaWiki creator Brooke Vibber. Here is what the strike threat means. Wikimedia cut 6 staff and roughly 600 volunteer editors signed a petition, threatening an editorial strike in protest. Wikipedia editors are threatening to strike because the Wikimedia Foundation laid off 6 staff, disbanded its Community Tech team, and fired MediaWiki creator Brooke Vibber, who volunteers say was a union organizer. As The Verge reported, roughly 600 volunteer editors signed a solidarity petition within hours, pledging collective action up to an editorial strike. Former Foundation employee Jake Orlowitz, who founded the Wikipedia Library, wrote that most of those let go were active in an emerging staff union and compared the moves to \"union-busting tactics typical of large IT companies.\" The Foundation denies that framing and says it is decentralizing technical work to reduce bottlenecks. ## Why Are Volunteer Editors Threatening to Strike?","score":0.8679453,"raw_content":null},{"url":"https://www.beutlerink.com/blog/wikiwise/editor-strikes-wikipedia-might","title":"Editor strikes and Wikipedia might","content":"Jun 17, 2026 — Last month, the Wikimedia Foundation dissolved its Community Tech team, a group of developers tasked with responding directly to the wishes ...Read more","score":0.7491794,"raw_content":null},{"url":"https://gigazine.net/gsc_news/en/20260625-uk-wikipedia-wikimedia-foundation-union","title":"Wikimedia Foundation employees send letters to ...","content":"Jun 25, 2026 — In May 2026, Brooke Vibber, former lead developer of MediaWiki and leader of a labor union, was fired, sparking criticism that the move was an","score":0.7161595,"raw_content":null},{"url":"https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Wikipedia:Wiki_Workers_United_solidarity","title":"Wikipedia:Wiki Workers United solidarity - Wikipedia","content":"> We, the undersigned, **stand in solidarity** with Wiki Workers United and affirm our willingness to engage in **collective action** if called upon by WWU, up to and including staging an **editorial strike**. Editors participating in the collective action would be able to use normal Wikipedia consensus-building methods to establish the action's terms and, if desired, to establish further demands of our own in addition to WWU's demands. It began as a direct response to the Wikimedia Foundation laying off the five engineers and a manager on the Community Tech team, most of whom were members of Wiki Workers United, a prospective union at the Foundation; the Foundation had also terminated Brooke Vibber—its most influential MediaWiki developer and a prominent WWU member—a week before. This petition is a statement of solidarity with WWU. It is separate from **meta:Wiki Workers United § Solidarity**, a more general pledge of solidarity that **signatories of this petition are also encouraged to sign**.","score":0.6742441,"raw_content":null},{"url":"https://medium.com/regarding-wikipedia/wikipedia-is-doing-the-capitalist-thing-56a393232943","title":"Big Tech's Anti-Labor Playbook Has Come for Wikipedia","content":"In mid-May, the Wikimedia Foundation fired Brooke Vibber. on May 21, the Foundation announced it had disbanded the Community Tech team.","score":0.59372765,"raw_content":null}],"response_time":0.63,"request_id":"b5b3bbe8-7285-44f2-9d3d-f60346f2bc3e"}
